 {"id":"bd-4sxh","title":"Code smell: Issue struct still has ~100 fields - consider composition","description":"internal/types/types.go Issue struct (lines 14-100) still has approximately 100 fields across many categories despite prior cleanup:

- Core identification (ID, ContentHash)
- Content (Title, Description, Design)
- Status & Workflow
- Assignment, Timestamps
- External integration
- Compaction metadata
- Relational data (Labels, Dependencies, Comments)
- Tombstone, Messaging, Context markers
- Bonding, HOP, Gate, Source tracing, Agent identity fields

**Problem:**
- Very large struct is difficult to understand
- Hard to identify required vs optional fields
- High change risk

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Group related fields into embedded structs (IssueMetadata, IssueCompaction, IssueAgent, etc.)
- [ ] Use composition instead of single flat struct
- [ ] Maintain backward compatibility for serialization
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"hooked","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:00.560267-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.353922-08:00","close_reason":"Refactored Issue struct using composition: created 4 embedded structs (IssueCompaction, IssueTombstone, IssueGate, IssueAgent) to group ~19 specialized fields while keeping core fields flat for ergonomic struct literals. JSON serialization unchanged.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4sxh","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.780801-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4t7","title":"Auto-import runs during --no-auto-import operations via stats/ready commands","description":"Even when using --no-auto-import flag, certain commands like 'bd stats' and 'bd ready' still trigger auto-import internally, which can cause the git history backfill bug to corrupt data.

Example:
  bd stats --no-auto-import
  # Still prints 'Purged bd-xxx (recovered from git history...)'

The flag should completely disable auto-import for the command, but it appears some code paths still trigger it.

Workaround: Use --allow-stale instead, or --sandbox mode.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:59.305898-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:20.335013-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T00:54:12.561872-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-4u2b","title":"Make MCP compaction settings configurable (COMPACTION_THRESHOLD, PREVIEW_COUNT)","description":"Currently COMPACTION_THRESHOLD (20) and PREVIEW_COUNT (5) are hardcoded constants at the module level. This prevents users from tuning context engineering behavior.

## Current State
```python
COMPACTION_THRESHOLD = 20  # Compact results with more than 20 issues
PREVIEW_COUNT = 5          # Show first 5 issues in preview
```

## Problems
1. No way for MCP clients to request different preview sizes
2. No way to disable compaction for debugging
3. No way to adapt to different context window sizes
4. Values are not documented in tool schema

## Recommended Solution
Add environment variables or MCP tool parameters:
- `BEADS_MCP_COMPACTION_THRESHOLD` env var (default: 20)
- `BEADS_MCP_PREVIEW_COUNT` env var (default: 5)
- Optional `compact_threshold` and `preview_size` parameters to list/ready tools

## Implementation Notes
- Keep current defaults for backward compatibility
- Read from environment at server initialization
- Document in CONTEXT_ENGINEERING.md
- Add tests verifying env var behavior","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:24:34.037997-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:40:48.79752-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-14T14:40:48.79752-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4u2b","depends_on_id":"bd-otf4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:24:34.039165-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4uoc","title":"Code Review Followup Summary: PR #481 + PR #551","description":"## Merged PRs Summary

### PR #551: Persist close_reason to issues table
- ✅ Merged successfully
- ✅ Bug fix: close_reason now persisted in database column (not just events table)
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage added
- ✅ Handles reopen case (clearing close_reason)

**Followup Issues Filed:**
- bd-lxzx: Document close_reason in JSONL export format
- bd-077e: Update CLI documentation for close_reason field

---

### PR #481: Context Engineering Optimizations (80-90% context reduction)
- ✅ Merged successfully  
- ✅ Lazy tool discovery: discover_tools() + get_tool_info()
- ✅ Minimal issue models: IssueMinimal (~80% smaller than full Issue)
- ✅ Result compaction: Auto-compacts results >20 items
- ✅ All 28 tests passing
- ⚠️ Breaking change: ready() and list() return type changed

**Followup Issues Filed:**
- bd-b318: Add integration tests for CompactedResult
- bd-4u2b: Make compaction settings configurable (THRESHOLD, PREVIEW_COUNT)
- bd-2kf8: Document CompactedResult response format in CONTEXT_ENGINEERING.md
- bd-pdr2: Document backwards compatibility considerations

---

## Overall Assessment

Both PRs are production-ready with solid implementations. All critical functionality works and tests pass. Followup issues focus on:
1. Documentation improvements (5 issues)
2. Integration test coverage (1 issue)
3. Configuration flexibility (1 issue)
4. Backwards compatibility guidance (1 issue)

No critical bugs or design issues found.

## Review Completed By
Code review process completed. @@ -860,7 +860,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-be7a","title":"Create npm package structure with package.json","description":"Set up initial npm package structure for @beads/bd:

## Files to create
- npm/package.json - Package metadata, dependencies, scripts
- npm/bin/bd - CLI wrapper script that invokes native binary
- npm/.gitignore - Ignore downloaded binaries
- npm/README.md - Installation and usage instructions

## package.json structure
- Name: @beads/bd (scoped package)
- Main: index.js (exports binary path)
- Bin: bin/bd (CLI entry point)
- Scripts: postinstall (download binary)
- Keywords: issue-tracker, cli, beads, bd
- License: MIT

## Bin wrapper
Simple Node.js script that:
- Spawns native binary with child_process.spawn
- Passes through all arguments and stdio
- Exits with binary's exit code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:39:47.416779-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T10:31:45.381258-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-be7a","depends_on_id":"bd-febc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-02T23:40:32.923859-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
